article_title: Square Enix Reveals Final Fantasy VII Revelation at Summer Game Fest, the Trilogy's Spring 2027 Finale Launching Day One on PS5, Switch 2, Xbox, and PC
summary: Accurate, fully sourced FF7 Revelation reveal; all quotes/specifics verified verbatim against snapshots. Two advisory warnings (allowlist membership, 1-char title overage) are non-factual and not correctable, so APPROVE rather than APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.

content_preview.title: Square Enix Reveals Final Fantasy VII Revelation at Summer Game Fest, the Trilogy's Spring 2027 Finale Launching Day One on PS5, Switch 2, Xbox, and PC
content_preview.summary: The third and final chapter of the Final Fantasy VII Remake trilogy was unveiled at Summer Game Fest with a simultaneous all-platform launch set for spring 2027.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Square Enix used Summer Game Fest to reveal **Final Fantasy VII Revelation**, the third and final entry in its Final Fantasy VII Remake trilogy. According to [RPG Site](https://www.rpgsite.net/news/20542-final-fantasy-vii-remake-part-3-revelation-ff7r-part-3), the game was announced during Summer Game Fest Live as "the third and final entry in the epic, critically acclaimed Final Fantasy VII Remake Series." [Kotaku](https://kotaku.com/final-fantasy-7-remake-part-3-is-officially-titl...

recommendations: Consider adding rpgsite.net, tweaktown.com, and pushsquare.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — all three are established gaming outlets and recurred in this beat.

editor_notes.content_quality: Clean, well-structured News piece (625 words, within the 400-1200 range). Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis structure with one claim per cited source. Appropriate technical depth for a game-reveal story.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 source snapshots read from disk and verified. source-0 (Kotaku, 200): confirms 'finally revealed the third and final chapter in the Final Fantasy 7 Remake trilogy', the 'resolve' theme quote verbatim, Matt Mercer onstage, and the Highwind/parachute open-world detail verbatim. source-1 (RPG Site, 200): confirms title 'Final Fantasy VII Revelation', 'third and final entry in the epic, critically acclaimed Final Fantasy VII Remake Series', Spring 2027, and the full platform list (PS5, Switch 2, Xbox Series X|S, PC via Steam/Epic/Xbox PC). source-2 (Engadget, 200): confirms 'will launch across all platforms in Spring 2027', 'simultaneously on PS5, Switch 2, Xbox and PC', the Highwind/parachute description, 'Cid and Vincent joining party battles properly for the first time', and the FITS equipment system quote verbatim. source-3 (TweakTown, 200): confirms 'launch Spring 2027, with a simultaneous cross-platform release on consoles and PC, including Xbox and even the Nintendo Switch 2', the Hamaguchi 'I too have carried a promise in my heart in this journey, the resolve to see this series through to the very end' quote verbatim, and 'most expansive open world yet'. source-4 (Push Square, 200): confirms the April Hamaguchi quote 'We know many fans are eagerly waiting, and preparations toward the announcement are steadily underway. Please look forward to it just a little longer.' verbatim. No snapshot failures; no WebFetch fallback needed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every quote, platform, name (Hamaguchi, Matt Mercer, Vincent, Cid), title, theme, and the Spring 2027 window traces to a cited snapshot. The release date is correctly hedged as 'spring 2027' with no fabricated specific day; 'What We Don't Know' explicitly states Square Enix did not narrow the window. The PlayStation-exclusivity break is accurately framed and cross-referenced.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The Analysis section frames the multiplatform strategy without sensationalism. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from the existing 2026-06/08 Final Fantasy VII Rebirth article, which covers Part 2's June 3 multiplatform launch and Metacritic score. This piece is the dedicated Part 3 (Revelation) reveal. The in-body cross-reference link resolves to the existing Rebirth article. No duplication.
recommendations: rpgsite.net, tweaktown.com, and pushsquare.com are not in the allowlist but are established gaming outlets; consider allowlisting. Title is 151 chars (1 over the 150 advisory soft cap) — cosmetic, not a factual issue.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully verified submission. The script's two warnings are advisory (allowlist membership and a 1-character title overage), neither a factual error nor anything a corrections note could honestly document. Verdict overridden to APPROVE.
